clinical_history: ECG from a middle-aged woman. What is the rhythm?
diagnosis: The ECG shows atrial fibrillation with a relatively rapid ventricular response at rest (mean rate: about 115; beat-to-beat range of about 75-150/min). A single probable ventricular premature complex (PVC) is present (third beat). Slow R wave progression in leads V1-V3 is non-diagnostic; a prior anterior infarct cannot be excluded. Borderline voltage criteria (Cornell) for left ventricular (LV) hypertrophy (confirmed with echocardiogram) are present, consistent with a prior history of systemic hypertension. Borderline leftward QRS axis deviation is present. Non-specific ST-T changes are present. The patient had a history of multivessel coronary artery disease, with well-preserved LV function.
